title: Cryptographic Randomness, Entropy, Keys, Salts, and Nonces · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Csprng Source
summary: A service seeds math/rand with time, generates keys and reset tokens from it, retries by reusing a nonce, and cannot prove fork or startup entropy behavior. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the csprng source increment in randomness and uniqueness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Use current Go cryptographic randomness APIs for keys, tokens, salts, nonces, and blinding while handling failure according to the API contract.
Define injective length-delimited serialization and reject ambiguous or non-canonical inputs before signing or authenticating.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
